by_judge.judge: M.NAGAPRASANNA IS NOT HOLDING SITTING ON 23.07.2026.

narrative:
On July 23, 2026, the Karnataka High Court recorded an overall hearing rate of 42.0%, resolving 1,005 out of 2,392 scheduled cases across 39 judges. This reflects a marginal improvement from the previous day's 40.9% conversion rate (1,007 of 2,464 cases), though it remains below the 46.6% clearance rate logged on July 21.

Procedurally, preliminary hearings demonstrated the highest conversion rate among high-volume stages at 55.0% (188 of 342 cases), followed by admission matters at 46.6% (193 of 414). Case listings scheduled for orders comprised the largest single category with 892 matters, converting 40.8% (364 cases) into active hearings. Conversely, final hearing matters registered lower progression, converting just 24.4% (113 of 463 scheduled).

In case-type distribution, Writ Petitions (WP) constituted the primary caseload with 973 scheduled matters and an improved hearing rate of 42.5%, rising from 37.6% on July 22 and 41.5% on July 21. Miscellaneous First Appeals (MFA) followed with 165 of 366 cases heard (45.1%). However, Criminal Petitions (CRL.P) experienced a notable drop in clearance, falling to 23.3% (64 of 275) compared to 50.4% yesterday and 51.0% two days prior.

Individual judge highlights included strong efficiency from Justice V. Srishananda in Court Hall 21, who completed 75.0% of listed cases (60 of 80), and Justice Suraj Govindaraj in Court Hall 11, who heard 76.5% (52 of 68). The division bench of Justices Anu Sivaraman and Venkatesh Naik T also maintained effective throughput, hearing 71.2% (42 of 59) of its main list in Court Hall 2. High listing burdens impacted output elsewhere, with Justice S. Vishwajith Shetty handling 99 of 281 scheduled matters (35.2%).
